description: Speaker consistency evaluation protocol for NepTTS-Bench. Defines metrics and thresholds for verifying that the distilled student model preserves the teacher's speaker identity, pitch characteristics, and speaking rate across all conditions.

notes.usage: Run speaker embedding extraction on all evaluation utterances. Compare teacher-vs-student and student-vs-student pairs. Evaluate F0 and speaking rate consistency across the stratified evaluation set.
notes.evaluation: A consistent model should sound like the same speaker regardless of utterance content, emotion, length, or language. Cross-condition tests verify identity preservation under variation.
notes.reference: Speaker verification thresholds calibrated against VoxCeleb benchmarks and recent TTS distillation literature (Seed-TTS-Eval, CosyVoice).

speaker_embedding_similarity.description: Cosine similarity between speaker embeddings extracted from utterance pairs. Measures whether two utterances sound like the same speaker.

teacher_vs_student.description: Same text spoken by teacher (Gemini) and student model. Measures how well the student preserves the teacher's voice identity.

student_vs_student.description: Different texts spoken by the student model. Measures internal voice consistency — the model should always sound like the same speaker.

f0_consistency.description: Fundamental frequency (pitch) consistency between teacher and student. Ensures the student model matches the teacher's pitch range and variability.
f0_consistency.extraction_method: CREPE or PYIN (on voiced segments only)

mean_f0.description: Mean fundamental frequency across voiced segments

std_f0.description: Standard deviation of fundamental frequency (pitch variability)

f0_consistency.comparison: Per-utterance: student F0 stats compared to teacher F0 stats for the same text

speaking_rate_consistency.description: Speaking rate measured as syllables per second. Student should match the teacher's pacing.
speaking_rate_consistency.measurement: Total syllable count (from text) divided by speech duration (excluding leading/trailing silence)

cross_condition_tests.description: Verify speaker identity is preserved across different speaking conditions. The model should sound like the same speaker regardless of what it says or how it says it.

emotion.description: Speaker consistency across emotion categories (neutral, happy, sad, urgent, calm)
emotion.test: Compute pairwise speaker embedding similarity across all emotion pairs. All pairs should meet student-vs-student thresholds.

utterance_length.description: Speaker consistency across short (<3s), medium (3-8s), and long (>8s) utterances
utterance_length.test: Compare speaker embeddings between length buckets. Longer utterances should not drift from short-utterance voice identity.

language.description: Speaker consistency between Nepali and English utterances (same voice, bilingual)
language.test: Compare speaker embeddings between Nepali-only and English-only utterances. The same voice should be recognizable in both languages.

evaluation_protocol.description: 200-utterance stratified evaluation set for speaker consistency testing.
procedure: Generate teacher audio for all 200 utterances using the selected Gemini voice
Generate student audio for the same 200 utterances
Extract ECAPA-TDNN speaker embeddings for all 400 audio files
Compute teacher-vs-student cosine similarity for each utterance pair (200 pairs)
Compute student-vs-student cosine similarity for 500 random student pairs
Extract F0 contours for all 400 audio files
Compute per-utterance F0 mean and std deviation comparison
Compute speaking rate for all 400 audio files
Report aggregate statistics and per-condition breakdowns

aggregate_reporting.description: How to report speaker consistency results.
visualizations: F0 mean scatter plot (teacher vs student)
Speaking rate scatter plot (teacher vs student)
